title: Signal Scope
description: Per-pair scope registry for indicator-axis and target-axis derivatives permitted on a pair's portal pages. Governs `results/{pair_id}/signal_scope.json`. Single source of truth enforcing ECON-SD (Pair Scope Discipline) and feeding ECON-UD (Universe Disclosure Methodology tables). Per META-CF (Contract File Standard) — producer (Evan) validates before save; consumers (Vera for chart rendering, Ace for Methodology-page tables, Quincy for QA audit) validate before use via `scripts/validate_schema.py`. Only mathematical derivatives of the single named indicator column (indicator_axis) and the single named target column (target_axis) are permitted. Non-derivative signals (alternative indicators, alternative targets) are scope violations per ECON-SD and cannot appear on the pair's page.

pair_id.description: Canonical pair identifier (snake_case, e.g. `hy_ig_v2_spy`). Matches the `results/{pair_id}/` directory name and the Analysis Brief pair slug.

schema_version.description: Semver of this instance. Bumped per META-CF on structural evolution of the scope registry. Minor bumps are additive (new derivatives appended); major bumps imply a renamed or removed derivative, which is a METAVNC-trackable change.

owner.description: Single agent accountable for this scope instance. Defaults to `evan`; other agents propose via Analyst Suggestions (ECON-AS) path.

last_updated_by.description: Agent code of the last editor (`dana` / `evan` / `vera` / `ray` / `ace` / `quincy`). Separate from `owner` — owner is accountable, last_updated_by is the actual editor (typically matches owner).

last_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time of the last edit to this instance.

indicator_axis.description: Indicator-axis block: the single named indicator column plus every mathematical derivative computed from it that is permitted on the pair's pages (ECON-SD).

target_axis.description: Target-axis block: the single named target column plus every mathematical derivative computed from it that is permitted on the pair's pages (ECON-SD).

notes.description: Freeform notes about this scope instance — e.g., retro-fit context, stakeholder-feedback references, known-gap disclosures.

axis_block.description: One side of the pair's scope — either indicator_axis or target_axis. Contains the single canonical column name, the display label, and the full list of permitted derivatives.

canonical_column.description: Exact parquet column name for the raw series (e.g. `hy_ig_spread_bps`, `spy_close`). This is the SINGLE column permitted as the raw input on this axis; any derivative must be computed from this column alone.

display_name.description: Human-readable label used on portal captions and Methodology-page tables (e.g. `HY-IG Credit Spread`, `SPY Total Return`).

derivatives.description: Ordered list of permitted derivatives on this axis. The raw column itself is included as the first entry with role='raw'. Subsequent entries are mathematical derivatives computed from that single column. Deduplicated by `name`.

derivative_entry.description: One permitted derivative on an axis. Rendered as a single row in the ECON-UD Universe Disclosure table on the Methodology page.

name.description: Derivative identifier (snake_case). Used internally and rendered in the Methodology-page table `name` column.

definition.description: Plain-English one-sentence description (META-ELI5 compliant — no formulas, no jargon without inline translation).

formula.description: Readable mathematical formula or pointer to source column. Example: `(x_t - mean_252(x)) / std_252(x)` for a z-score; `ln(p_{t+21}) - ln(p_t)` for a 21-day forward log return; `raw column` for the un-transformed series.

role.description: Functional role of the derivative. `raw` = un-transformed source column (one per axis); `derivative` = mathematical transformation; `threshold_input` = fed to a strategy threshold rule; `regime_state` = HMM / Markov state label or probability; `diagnostic` = considered for diagnostic output only (not rendered as a primary chart trace).

appears_in_charts.description: List of chart names (drawn from VIZ-V8 chart_type_registry) on which this derivative is rendered. Empty array = considered but not charted (still disclosed on the Methodology Universe table for transparency).

notes.description: Freeform notes — sign convention, lookback window caveats, stationarity remarks, known pitfalls.
